creator: bobf@mrp3.com
text:
ports tree was already up to date at the time of the report.

following re-boot build problem disappeared.  At the time I was 
attempting 'portmaster -a' and portmaster unforgivingly stopped at this 
point, so I corrected it as best as I could and continued forward. 
Later I had other issues and switched to 'portupgrade -a' so that I 
could continue as far as possible without stopping/re-starting 
indefinitely.  Once I resolved all of the various problems (most of 
which seemed to be corrected after doing another re-build of gettext 
dependent packages) and doing a series of 'pkgdb -fF' operations I 
finally ended up at a point where everything built and ran.  After this 
I re-booted and the original build problem with inkscape disappeared.

Apparently there were so many different updates to ports since June 2010 
that they conflicted with one another.  It was not possible simply to 
follow the instructions and go from there (there were too many things 
that simply would not build properly unless everything was already 
correct).  This must have been one of those.

My amd64 system ( which last had ports built from July 2010 ) did not 
appear to have this problem during the upgrade process.  However, I 
still ended up doing a forced dependency re-build on a different library 
to correct a different (dependency-related) problem (which may have 
masked this problem).  It is also a newer system that has only gone 
through the upgrade process a couple of times.

attaching env output and pkg_info output.
